task_completion.description: Open the initial wind turbine project in the simulation software
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade GUI is open and shows the loaded project 'start_turbine.wpa' from /home/ga/Documents/projects/.

task_completion.description: Conduct the baseline BEM simulation with specified parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Simulation setup or results screen in QBlade showing a BEM simulation run with Wind Speed=10m/s, TSR=7, and Pitch=0, displaying a baseline Power Coefficient (Cp) value.

task_completion.description: Refine the blade discretization to at least 50 sections
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blade design or geometry modification screen showing the number of blade sections/elements increased to 50 or more.

task_completion.description: Conduct the refined BEM simulation with the same parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Simulation setup or results screen showing a second BEM simulation run on the refined blade with Wind Speed=10m/s, TSR=7, and Pitch=0, displaying a new refined Power Coefficient (Cp) value.

task_completion.description: Save the refined project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager showing the project being saved as 'refined_turbine.wpa' in the /home/ga/Documents/projects/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create a report containing both baseline and refined Cp values
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing a file named 'mesh_study_report.txt' in /home/ga/Documents/projects/ that contains two distinct Power Coefficient (Cp) values corresponding to the baseline and refined simulations.

integrity.description: Agent used the QBlade GUI to perform the simulations and blade modifications
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the QBlade graphical interface (clicking menus, entering parameters in dialogs) rather than just running terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The Cp values in the report match the actual simulation results
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into 'mesh_study_report.txt' match the Cp values visibly generated in the QBlade simulation result screens, proving they were not fabricated.

integrity.description: Agent actually modified the blade mesh rather than just running the same simulation twice
integrity.visual_evidence: There is clear visual evidence of the blade section count being modified in the blade design interface before the second simulation is run.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Start project path: /home/ga/Documents/projects/start_turbine.wpa. Expected refined project path: /home/ga/Documents/projects/refined_turbine.wpa. Report file path: /home/ga/Documents/projects/mesh_study_report.txt. Target simulation parameters: Wind Speed=10m/s, TSR=7, Pitch=0. The refined blade must have at least 50 sections (though underlying verifier logic may accept >= 48, look for >= 50 as per the task description).
